title: Indulgent Charred Norwegian Lamination-puff Galette
description: A rich and decadent Norwegian dessert featuring layers of lamination-puff pastry, topped with a charred and caramelized cranberry glaze.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll out the lamination-puff pastry to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm) and cut into 4-6 squares depending on the size you prefer.

In a small saucepan, combine the cranberry juice, brown sugar, and unsalted butter. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the glaze has thickened slightly.
Brush the tops of the pastry squares with the heavy cream and sprinkle with granulated sugar.
Place the pastry squares on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown.
While the pastry is baking, whisk the egg and vanilla extract together in a small bowl.
Remove the pastry from the oven and brush with the egg mixture, then return to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.
Remove the pastry from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before topping with the charred and caramelized cranberry glaze.
Drizzle the cranberry glaze over the top of the pastry and serve warm, garnished with fresh berries if desired.
